## Fix storybook

You setup storybook in epic 8 and tried to fix them in epic 9 where you incorrectly diagnosed the issue as not occurring (see storybook-diagnosis-9-1.md) but all the stories have the same error:

Cannot destructure property 'id' of 'defaultExport' as it is undefined.

You obviously did not use playwright MCP server to verify the stories were working before merging the storybook setup. Nor did you do anything beyond verifying that they built properly. Please fix the storybook setup and verify all the stories are working.

One thing I notice is that you did not use @nx/storybook plugin and you should have because we are using NX. You can find the documentation for using @nx/storybook here: https://nx.dev/docs/technologies/test-tools/storybook/introduction.

You might want to undo the work you've already done and start from scratch with the @nx/storybook plugin to ensure that everything is set up correctly. Make sure to verify the stories are working using the playwright MCP server before merging any changes. By working, displaying the component, not that it just renders something.

## The wait spinner is no longer centered on the screen

When I clicked the "Refresh" button in the Screener screen, the wait spinner appeared in the upper left corner of the screen instead of centered in the screen. You tried to fix this once in Epic 10. This is probably because of the refactor to Tailwind CSS that occurred in Epic 8. You can look at the commit history to see what styles were being used before. All the wait spinners have the same issue because they all use the same code. Please fix the wait spinner so that it is centered on the screen again. Use the Tailwind CSS utilities to center the spinner both vertically and horizontally on the screen. Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that the spinner is centered on the screen.

## The table layout in Account -> Dividend Deposits is broken

You tried to fix this in Epic 12 and you did manage to solve the problem in the Open Positions and Sold Positions tables but the Dividend Deposits table is better but still has some issues:

- It doesn't display any rows initially but it does display the scrollbar.
- Once I scroll to the bottom and back, I see rows, but when I scroll down again, the rows disappear again.
- The one thing that may make a difference is that the Dividend Deposits table only has one header row while the Open Positions and Sold Positions tables have two header rows.

## Global Summary layout

The layout of the Global Summary screen is still off. You tried to fix this in Epic 13. But it doesn't look any better.

When the page loads from a cold start, the line graph sits over (new layer, not vertically) the pie chart.

Later it looks like I describe below.

The line graph should be to the right of the summary information and pie chart. The line graph is currently below the summary information. This is probably because of the refactor to Tailwind CSS that occurred in Epic 8. You can look at the commit history to see what styles were being used before.

Please fix the layout of the Global Summary screen so that the line graph is to the right of the summary information. Use Tailwind CSS utilities to create a responsive layout that places the line graph to the right of the summary information on larger screens and stacks them vertically on smaller screens. Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that the layout of the Global Summary screen is correct on different screen sizes.

I recommend checking out the code just before Epic 8 to see how the layout looked when it was working correctly and then use Tailwind CSS to replicate that layout.

## Account Summary layout

The layout of the Account Summary screen is also still off. But now it is off in the same way that the Global Summary screen is off. Once you get the Global Summary screen layout fixed, the Account Summary screen layout should be fixed using a similar mechanism. Please verify that the layout of the Account Summary screen is correct after you fix the Global Summary screen layout. Use the Playwright MCP server to verify that the layout of the Account Summary screen is correct.
